topic: Glob Grep and file discovery tools in AI agent harnesses
scope: How harnesses expose filesystem search to LLMs: Claude Code Glob/Grep, subagents, Codex/OpenAI shell + file_search, Aider repo-map, Cline list/search, OpenCode grep/glob, anti-patterns, ripgrep gotchas, pagination.

sources.title: Claude Code overview
keyInsights: Claude Code is an agentic coding environment with first-class file read, edit, and shell tools
Each surface (CLI, VS Code, JetBrains, Web) connects to the same underlying engine and tool set

sources.title: Best Practices for Claude Code
keyInsights: Context window performance degrades as it fills; file reads dominate consumption
Subagents run in separate context windows and return summaries, preserving main context
Kitchen-sink sessions, over-correction, over-specified CLAUDE.md are classic anti-patterns
Subagent example explicitly grants Read, Grep, Glob, Bash tools for security review

sources.title: Create custom subagents
keyInsights: Subagents preserve main-context tokens by isolating search/exploration work
Claude uses subagent descriptions to decide when to delegate
Each subagent can have its own restricted tool list and model (e.g. Haiku)

sources.title: Explore the context window
keyInsights: File reads dominate context usage (~2400 tokens for a typical auth file)
A grep of 'refreshToken' across the codebase costs ~600 tokens
Startup loads: system prompt (~4200), CLAUDE.md files, auto memory, MCP tool names
MCP tool schemas are deferred by default; agents load specific ones via tool search

sources.title: Ripgrep user guide
keyInsights: --type/-t filters by file type (js, py, rust...); -T excludes a type
-g glob filter: '*.toml' include, '!*.toml' exclude; later globs override earlier
Output modes: -c count, -o only-matching, -C context lines, --sort path
Multiline: -U enables cross-line patterns; PCRE2 multiline requires full-file memory load
Respects .gitignore/.ignore/.rgignore by default; -u/-uu/-uuu progressively disables
Use -F for literal string matching without regex interpretation

sources.title: Ripgrep FAQ
keyInsights: Multi-line mode (-U) disables parallelism and can be slow on large files
Default engine cannot do lookaround/backreferences; use -P for PCRE2
For ASCII-only PCRE2 patterns use --no-pcre2-unicode for best performance
Windows Cygwin mangles paths starting with /; escape or set MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1
Oh My Zsh Rails plugin creates conflicting 'rg' alias

sources.title: Ripgrep homepage
keyInsights: Three design principles: smart defaults, performance through optimization, Unicode-first
Recursive search respecting .gitignore is the default mode
SIMD-accelerated literal extraction + finite-automaton matching
Type filtering and .gitignore compliance are critical for automation tools

sources.title: Rust regex crate syntax
keyInsights: Meta-characters needing escape: { } [ ] ( ) . * + ? ^ $ | \
Unicode is on by default; . matches scalar values, not just ASCII
Character-class set ops: intersection &&, difference --, symmetric diff ~~
m flag changes ^/$ to match line boundaries

sources.title: Aider repository map
keyInsights: Repo-map sends important classes/functions + signatures with each user turn
Graph ranking algorithm selects most relevant portions within --map-tokens budget (default 1000)
Map expands automatically when no files are added to chat

sources.title: Aider slash commands
keyInsights: /add and /drop manage file inclusion manually
/ls lists all known files and indicates which are in chat
/map prints the current repo map
/read-only adds reference files that aider won't edit

sources.title: Aider usage tips
keyInsights: Be selective with /add; too much irrelevant code distracts the LLM
For new files, run /add first so Aider doesn't overwrite existing files
Chunked workflow: break large goals into smaller sequential tasks

sources.title: Aider repomap.py implementation
keyInsights: Uses tree-sitter parsers (per-language .scm query files) for semantic tag extraction
networkx.pagerank() ranks definitions by graph centrality
Files mentioned in chat get 50x rank multiplier; 8+ char identifiers get 10x boost
Caches parsed state in diskcache.Cache keyed on mtime

sources.title: Cline tools guide
keyInsights: Four read-side tools: read_file, list_files, search_files, list_code_definition_names
search_files uses path + regex + file_pattern (XML invocation shape)
list_code_definition_names is distinct: extracts function/class definitions

sources.title: Cline complete tool reference
keyInsights: Full tool surface: read_file, list_files, search_files, list_code_definition_names, write_to_file, replace_in_file
execute_command for shell; browser_action via Puppeteer
use_mcp_tool / access_mcp_resource for MCP extensions

sources.title: OpenCode built-in tools
keyInsights: Tools: grep, glob, read, edit, write, bash, lsp, apply_patch, skill, todowrite, webfetch, websearch, question
glob returns paths sorted by modification time (newest first)
read supports line-range specifications for large files
No dedicated list tool; glob + read cover the space

sources.title: OpenCode grep tool source
keyInsights: Parameters: pattern (regex), path (optional, default cwd), include (optional glob)
Backed by ripgrep via internal file/ripgrep module
Results grouped by file, sorted by mtime desc, capped at 100 matches
Line text >2000 chars truncated with '...'; header shows truncation state

sources.title: OpenCode glob tool source
keyInsights: Parameters: pattern (required), path (optional)
Results sorted b.mtime - a.mtime (newest first)
Caps at 100 files with truncation notice
Permission checks validate path is a directory

sources.title: OpenCode repository
keyInsights: Two built-in agents: 'build' (full-access) and 'plan' (read-only)
'general' subagent handles complex searches
Open-source harness modeled on Claude Code architecture

sources.title: OpenAI Codex CLI
keyInsights: Lightweight coding agent running in the terminal
Rust-based implementation
Can read, change, and run code in a selected directory

sources.title: Codex sandbox approvals
keyInsights: Pre-approved read-only commands: rg, ls, find, grep
--ask-for-approval untrusted runs only known-safe read operations automatically
State-mutating commands always require approval
Sandbox approach: enforce security at shell layer, not by narrowing tool surface

sources.title: Codex CLI config
keyInsights: MCP servers configured in ~/.codex/config.toml
supports_parallel_tool_calls enables parallel execution per server
mcp_servers section stores approval defaults and per-tool overrides

sources.title: Anthropic: Building Effective Agents
keyInsights: Three principles: simplicity, transparency, careful ACI design
Treat tool definitions like docstrings for junior developers
Poka-yoke tool design: make errors hard to commit
Workflows (predefined paths) vs Agents (LLM-directed); pick the simplest that works
Absolute filepaths prevented navigation errors in SWE-bench

sources.title: Anthropic: Multi-agent Research System
keyInsights: Vague subagent instructions cause duplicated work; use structured decomposition
LeadResearcher persists plan to Memory since context >200k tokens gets truncated
Subagents store outputs externally, pass lightweight references back
Fresh-context handoffs for conversations spanning hundreds of turns
